_principle: Declarative NLU patterns. Each entry is a named regex used by the deterministic extractors / guards to detect customer intent before the LLM is called. Centralising them here means: product owners can review/tweak phrasing without TS changes, new languages can be added by extending an entry (use byLanguage), and the regex is validated at boot. Anti-hardcode rule: any 'detect customer intent X by regex' MUST live here, never as a const RegExp in TS. Consumers go through utils/nlu.ts.

patterns.description: Known recoverable display tokens + alarm family (SEL/PUSH/DOOR/ALM*/ALN*/AL001/001/END/FILTRO/STOP/water). Matches when the customer types one of these as the WHOLE message.
Generic ERR codes: ERR + 1-3 digits with optional space/dash separator (ERR 52, ERR-52, ERR52).
Catch-all uppercase code 4-15 chars (ERROR, FAIL, TIMEOUT). Case-sensitive on purpose (lowercase `error` is conversational, not a display).
Uppercase yes/no tokens that should NOT be classified as a display state. Used by shouldAcceptAsDisplay() to short-circuit before the display-token whitelist.
Punctuation stripped before display-token classification. Replaces matching characters with empty string.
Customer pivots to a payment incident: datafono / TPV / cobro+€. Triggers Caso 21-22 family + datafono-wrong-amount.
Customer pivots to an ops incident requiring camera / AJAX / technical support review.
Customer added more coins/money to a dryer but minutes did not increase (Caso 21 Alemanya, Caso 22 Pineda).
Customer cannot pay with the card (Caso 23 Alemanya, Caso 24 Hortes).
Generic 'wait, actually' particle that signals a topic pivot. Combined with topicCorrectionContext to trigger a switch even without a strong topic signal.
Domain words that, paired with switchHint, confirm the customer is correcting their previous narrative toward a different topic.
